message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: credentials-and-history.

Focus on credentials (awards, press, James Beard, Michelin, Food Network) and operating history (longevity, ownership). Try to REFUTE each claim. Look up each award independently — James Beard winners and nominees are listed in primary sources at jamesbeard.org. Michelin is listed at guide.michelin.com. Cross-check year-by-year.

Entity under review: Copley's on Palm Canyon (Palm Springs)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Tripadvisor 1,057 reviews (4.5/5, #12 of 281 Palm Springs); Yelp 1,079 reviews (4.2); OpenTable cited at ~6,437 diners (4.8) per third-party summary [UNVERIFIED-direct]; Google ~4.4
- Sentiment: mostly positive
- Longevity: Opened December 2004; same owner-operators (Andrew Manion Copley, Juliana Manion Copley, Greg Butterfield) ~21 years — stable independent ownership
- Credentials: ["OpenTable 100 Most Romantic Restaurants in America (2024)","OpenTable Diners' Choice (2026)","OpenTable Top 100 Patio Dining in America [UNVERIFIED-year]","Restaurateurs of the Year — Desert Resorts Convention and Visitors' Authority [UNVERIFIED-year]","Chef Andrew Copley listed in James Beard Foundation chef archive (not an award)","Featured/cited in New York Times (described as 'emerging chef' / 'hottest place') [UNVERIFIED-direct]","Zagat ratings cited on own site [UNVERIFIED-direct]","Co-owner Juliana Copley: co-founder of Palm Springs Restaurant Week; president of Palm Springs Desert Resorts Restaurant Association"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces strongly for: 'date night Palm Springs', 'romantic restaurants Palm Springs', 'upscale Palm Springs dining', 'uptown design district restaurants', 'patio dining Palm Springs'. Surfaces moderately for: 'fine dining Palm Springs', 'best restaurants Palm Springs'. Does NOT consistently surface for: 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' (eclipsed by Spencer's, Workshop Kitchen, Le Vallauris, Mister Parker's, 4Saints), 'contemporary American Palm Springs' (mentioned alongside Eight4Nine, Trio, Jake's rather than as anchor). Notably absent from 2025 Michelin Recommended list for Palm Springs.
- Site structured data: Weak. copleyspalmsprings.com has NO JSON-LD / schema.org markup (no Restaurant, LocalBusiness, OpeningHoursSpecification, or Menu schema). Address (621 N Palm Canyon Drive) and phone (760-327-9555) appear as plain text and tel: link. Hours present only as human-readable prose ('Open at 5:30pm 7 Nights a Week through May'). Menu pages exist but not machine-parseable. Agents must rely on third-party aggregators (Yelp, OpenTable, Tripadvisor, Visit Greater Palm Springs) for structured data.
- Visibility gap score: 6
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "credentials-and-history"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the web search results, here's the information about Copley's on Palm Canyon:

## Opening and History

Copley's on Palm Canyon officially opened in December 2004, with Andrew serving as owner and executive chef and Juliana managing the business operations.

## Andrew Copley's Background

Chef Copley was born and raised in Birmingham, England, and he graduated from Westminster Culinary College in London. He began his career at the renowned Savoy and Park Lane hotels before taking his expertise global, earning accolades at the Grand Hyatt on Australia's Sunshine Coast and leading culinary teams at the Ritz Carlton in Maui and San Francisco.

## Restaurant Concept

The restaurant's historic location is once part of the estate of Hollywood legend Cary Grant. Andrew and Juliana immediately began renovating and restoring the 3,500-square-foot property, which was originally built sometime during the 1920s.

## Recognition

Chef Andrew Copley has been called an "emerging chef" by the New York Times and is a contributor to media such as Restaurant & Hospitality, Rachael Ray Magazine and NBC. Copley's has consistently been rated among the top restaurants in the valley by Zagat's and New York Times.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Based on the search results, I found information about Juliana Copley and her involvement with Palm Springs Restaurant Week:

Juliana is an active member of the Coachella Valley business community, where she serves as the president of the Palm Springs Desert Resorts Restaurant Association and was one of the founders of Palm Springs Restaurant Week.

Juliana's career as an entrepreneur began in 2001, when she co-founded a highly successful Destination Management Company in Hawaii, which she sold several years later when she moved to Palm Springs to open a restaurant with her husband Andrew Copley and good friend Greg Butterfield.

Copley's on Palm Canyon officially opened in December 2004, with Andrew serving as owner and executive chef and Juliana managing the business operations.

Based on the search results, I found relevant information about Copley's in Palm Springs and its recognition on OpenTable's 2024 list.

Copley's on Palm Canyon was ranked one of the top 100 romantic restaurants by OpenTable in 2024. OpenTable listed three Palm Springs restaurants in their top 100 most romantic restaurants in America: Copley's on Palm Canyon, FARM, and Le Vallauris.

OpenTable analyzed over 12 million diner reviews and metrics to find the restaurants that had diners swooning—these are the Top 100 Romantic Restaurants in the US. Copley's is an award-winning restaurant serving American cuisine infused with hints of tropical and European flavors. Located in part of the former Cary Grant estate in the heart of Palm Springs, guests experience an upscale and inviting dining experience either outside on the patio with stunning mountain views or by enjoying the friendly atmosphere indoors.

# OpenTable Top 100 Romantic Restaurants 2024 - Palm Springs

Yes, the article does list Copley's on Palm Canyon as one of the honorees.

**The three Palm Springs restaurants named are:**

1. **Copley's on Palm Canyon** - Described as "an award-winning restaurant serving upscale American cuisine infused with traces of tropical and European flavors" located on part of the former Cary Grant estate

2. **FARM** - Characterized as offering "an extraordinary sensory experience" with garden-like patio seating and "authentic menu comprised of the freshest ingredients"

3. **Le Vallauris** - Noted as "Located in a restored historic landmark" in Downtown Palm Springs, serving "traditional French food by chef Jean-Paul Lair"

The article states that OpenTable "analyzed over 12 million diner reviews and metrics" to identify these top romantic dining destinations.

Based on the search results, I found information about Copley's Palm Springs receiving the "Restaurateurs of the Year" award from the Desert Resorts Convention and Visitors' Authority:

Juliana and her husband Chef Andrew Copley were named "Restaurateurs of the Year" by the Desert Resorts Convention and Visitors' Authority. More specifically, the Palm Springs Desert Resort Communities Convention and Visitors Authority named Copley's on Palm Canyon 2009 Restaurateur of the Year among six additional nominees competing for the title.

The Hospitality Achievement Awards recognized restaurateurs, hoteliers, and hospitality business owners throughout the desert community who were nominated by peers, and the restaurant is owned by executive chef Andrew Copley and his wife, business manager Juliana Copley.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text: I have enough verification data. Let me finalize.

confirms: Opened December 2004 with Andrew Copley, Juliana Copley, and Greg Butterfield as owner-operators (~21 years stable independent ownership) — confirmed via visitpalmsprings.com and copleyspalmsprings.com/about
OpenTable Top 100 Most Romantic Restaurants in America 2024 — confirmed via PS Tribune (Feb 4, 2024) and Patch coverage listing Copley's alongside FARM and Le Vallauris
Juliana Copley is co-founder of Palm Springs Restaurant Week and president of Palm Springs Desert Resorts Restaurant Association — confirmed via visitpalmsprings.com profile
Restaurateur of the Year — Palm Springs Desert Resort Communities Convention & Visitors Authority — year is 2009 (per QSR Magazine), confirming the claim and resolving the [UNVERIFIED-year] flag
Chef Andrew Copley appears in the James Beard Foundation chef archive — confirmed via archive.jamesbeard.org/chef/andrew-copley, but it is purely a biographical listing with NO award or nomination

Notably absent from 2025 Michelin Recommended list for Palm Springs — confirmed; Michelin Recommended Palm Springs list (4Saints, Workshop Kitchen & Bar, The Colony Club, Cheeky's, Tac/Quila, etc.) does not include Copley's
contradicts: Restaurateurs of the Year [UNVERIFIED-year] should be updated: the year is 2009 per QSR Magazine — not currently a recent honor, so framing as a current credential would overstate recency
flagged_as_unverified: OpenTable Diners' Choice (2026) — not independently confirmed via OpenTable's official Diners' Choice list for 2026
OpenTable Top 100 Patio Dining in America — year still unverified; no specific year/list found in independent sources
New York Times 'emerging chef' / 'hottest place' citation — repeated only on the restaurant's own site and third-party summaries; no direct NYT article located
Zagat ratings cited on own site — Zagat as an active rating service has been largely defunct since ~2020; cited rating is self-reported with no current Zagat source
Review volume figures (Tripadvisor 1,057 / Yelp 1,079 / OpenTable 6,437 / Google 4.4) — not independently re-counted in this lens
input.notes: Core history (Dec 2004 opening, three-owner team, ~21 years) and the strongest current credential (OpenTable Top 100 Most Romantic 2024) hold up under independent verification. The James Beard 'archive chef' listing is correctly characterized as not an award — important not to upgrade this. Restaurateur of the Year is genuine but dates to 2009, so it's a legacy credential, not a recent one. NYT 'emerging chef' and Zagat citations remain self-attested and should stay flagged. Absence from 2025 Michelin Recommended list independently confirmed — supports the visibility gap framing.
